commit.message:
Plugin UI (#209)

* Fix sample load crash and residual synthesizer drowning out harmonics

Two bugs fixed:

1. Load button heap corruption: CTextButton with unregistered control-tag
   caused VSTGUI's ParameterChangeListener to corrupt the heap. Replaced
   with custom SampleLoadButton (CView subclass) following Ruinae's
   OutlineButton pattern — no tags, no parameter binding.

2. Residual synthesizer output 600,000x louder than harmonic oscillator:
   - Band energies (spectral envelope) were raw FFT magnitudes used as
     multipliers. Now normalized to unit RMS for shape-only control.
   - totalEnergy was at FFT-domain scale. Now divided by fftSize to
     convert to time-domain amplitude via Parseval's theorem.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add E2E tests for sample load → analysis → pitched resynthesis pipeline

5 tests covering the full WAV file → SampleAnalyzer → Processor → audio:
- SampleAnalyzer extracts f0 from sine WAV
- Resynthesized output matches MIDI note pitch
- Different MIDI notes produce different pitches (tuning)
- Multi-harmonic WAV preserves harmonic structure
- Output is tonal, not noise (spectral peak > broadband)

Includes Goertzel frequency detection and WAV file writer helpers.

* fix(generators): Fix false circular reference detection for primitives

The previous commit introduced primitive type handling in GetTypeInfoInternal,
but it incorrectly delegated to GetOrCreateTypeInfo<T>() which has its own
circular reference tracking. Since the caller (IJsonTypeInfoResolver.GetTypeInfo)
had already added the type to TypesBeingCreated, this caused a false "circular

of going through GetOrCreateTypeInfo<T>(). This avoids the redundant circular
reference check since primitives can never have actual circular references.

* fix(postgres): Cap exponential backoff at 5 minutes to prevent interval overflow

The exponential backoff calculation in failure processing functions
(process_outbox_failures, process_inbox_failures,
process_perspective_event_failures) could overflow PostgreSQL's
interval type when attempts count was high.

Error: 22008: interval out of range
Cause: POWER(2, attempts+1) becomes astronomically large

Solution: Cap the multiplier at 10 using LEAST(), giving a maximum
backoff of 5 minutes (30s * 10 = 300s). The backoff grows
exponentially (60s, 120s, 240s) then stays at 5 minutes.

Also adds test coverage for the capping behavior.

commit.message:
Hearth: Live activity panel (Forge-ono) (#51)

* fix: Hearth live activity panel — handle Gemini events and lifecycle worker logs

Two root causes for intermittent Live Activity display:

1. Gemini provider emits top-level tool_use events ({type:"tool_use",
   tool_name:"Read"}) that parseWorkerActivity didn't handle. Claude
   nests these inside assistant message content blocks. When workers
   ran on Gemini, tool calls (the majority of activity) were invisible.
   Also accumulate Gemini delta text fragments into single entries
   instead of one [text] per tiny fragment.

2. Lifecycle workers (cifix, reviewfix, rebase) never called
   UpdateWorkerLogPath after spawning their smith processes, so
   LogPath stayed empty in the state DB and the panel always showed
   "No activity" for these workers.

Fixes:
- Add tool_use/tool_result top-level event handling in parseWorkerActivity
- Accumulate Gemini message deltas before flushing as a single [text] entry
- Add WorkerID field to cifix.FixParams, reviewfix.FixParams, rebase.Params
- Call UpdateWorkerLogPath after smith spawn in all three lifecycle packages
- Pass workerID from daemon dispatcher to lifecycle Fix functions
- Add tests covering both Claude and Gemini log formats

Bead: Forge-ono
